questions.q: Who is the divine father of Hanuman in the Ramayana?
options: Indra, the king of the gods
questions.explanation: Hanuman is celebrated as the son of Vayu, the wind-god, and is often called Pavanaputra or Vayuputra.

questions.q: To which race of forest-dwelling beings does Hanuman belong in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is a vanara, one of the monkey-like beings who aid Rama in the Ramayana.

questions.q: Hanuman is renowned as the supreme devotee of which prince of Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is the foremost devotee of Rama, embodying selfless service and devotion throughout the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, across what great obstacle does Hanuman leap to reach Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman makes a mighty leap across the ocean to reach the island of Lanka in search of Sita.

questions.q: Whom does Hanuman search for and find when he reaches Lanka in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman crosses to Lanka to locate Sita, whom he finds held captive in the Ashoka grove.

questions.q: Which weapon is most commonly associated with Hanuman in his iconography?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is most often depicted carrying a mace, the gada, a symbol of his immense strength.

questions.q: In which kanda (book) of the Ramayana is Hanuman the central figure?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's exploits in Lanka form the heart of the Sundara Kanda, the fifth book of the Ramayana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which garden in Lanka is the grove where Hanuman finds Sita held captive?
questions.explanation: Hanuman discovers Sita imprisoned in the Ashoka Vatika, the grove of ashoka trees in Ravana's Lanka.

questions.q: Hanuman is revered as a chiranjivi, meaning he is which of the following?
options: An immortal who lives through the ages
questions.explanation: As a chiranjivi, Hanuman is believed to be immortal, living on through the ages by the grace he received.

questions.q: Hanuman is widely venerated as a giver of which qualities to his devotees?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is venerated as a bestower of strength, courage, and protection to those who honor him.

questions.q: Which mountain of healing herbs does Hanuman carry to revive the wounded Lakshmana in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Unable to identify the single herb, Hanuman lifts and carries the entire Sanjivani mountain to heal Lakshmana.

questions.q: By what name, meaning 'son of the wind,' is Hanuman commonly known?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is called Pavanaputra, 'son of the wind,' honoring his father Vayu, the wind-god.

questions.q: Who is the mother of Hanuman in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Hanuman's mother is Anjana, a celestial being, which is why he is also called Anjaneya, 'son of Anjana.'

questions.q: How does Hanuman set much of Lanka ablaze in the Ramayana?
options: With his tail, which the rakshasas had set on fire
questions.explanation: When the rakshasas set fire to Hanuman's tail, he uses the burning tail to set much of Lanka ablaze.

questions.q: When Hanuman first reveals himself to Sita in Lanka, what token of Rama does he give her?
questions.explanation: Hanuman shows Sita Rama's signet ring as proof that he is truly Rama's messenger.

questions.q: By what name, derived from his mother, is Hanuman often addressed?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is called Anjaneya, meaning 'son of Anjana,' after his mother Anjana.

questions.q: Which vanara king does Hanuman serve as a minister before joining the search for Sita?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is a trusted minister of Sugriva, the vanara king, and it is in his service that he first meets Rama.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, on whose behalf does Hanuman first approach Rama and Lakshmana in the forest?
questions.explanation: Hanuman approaches Rama and Lakshmana as an envoy of Sugriva, beginning the alliance that aids Rama.

questions.q: The popular forty-verse devotional hymn praising Hanuman, attributed to Tulsidas, is known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Hanuman Chalisa, a hymn of forty verses by Tulsidas, is among the most beloved devotional praises of Hanuman.

questions.q: In the Sundara Kanda, what does Hanuman do to his own body to enter Lanka unseen and to search its halls?
questions.explanation: Hanuman shrinks himself to a small size to slip into Lanka and search its palaces without being detected.

questions.q: Which mighty son of Ravana captures Hanuman in Lanka by binding him with the Brahmastra?
questions.explanation: Indrajit, also called Meghanada, binds Hanuman using the Brahmastra and brings him before Ravana's court.

questions.q: On which day of the week is Hanuman especially worshipped in popular Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is traditionally worshipped on Tuesdays, and also on Saturdays, in popular Hindu devotional practice.

questions.q: According to a well-known episode of Hanuman's childhood, which celestial body did the infant Hanuman leap toward, mistaking it for a fruit?
questions.explanation: As an infant, Hanuman leapt toward the sun, mistaking the glowing orb for a ripe fruit to eat.

questions.q: In a famous childhood tale, which deity strikes the infant Hanuman with a thunderbolt (vajra), injuring his jaw?
questions.explanation: Indra strikes the leaping infant Hanuman with his vajra, injuring his jaw, an event linked to the name 'Hanuman.'

questions.q: During his flight to Lanka, Hanuman is tested by Surasa, the mother of the nagas. How does he pass her in the Ramayana?
options: By outwitting her, shrinking to enter and exit her mouth
By flying high above the clouds
questions.explanation: Surasa, mother of the nagas, was sent by the gods to test Hanuman; he honors her boon by shrinking to a tiny form, darting into and out of her mouth, then passing on.

questions.q: Which she-rakshasa, dwelling in the sea and seizing shadows, does Hanuman slay during his flight to Lanka?
questions.explanation: Hanuman slays the shadow-seizing demoness Simhika, who tried to drag him down by his shadow during his ocean crossing.

questions.q: According to a traditional account, Hanuman is regarded as a partial incarnation or aspect of which great deity?
questions.explanation: In many traditions Hanuman is regarded as an amsha or incarnation of Shiva, also called Rudra.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, Hanuman appears to which Pandava brother, who is also a son of the wind-god Vayu?
questions.explanation: Hanuman meets Bhima in the forest; both are sons of Vayu, making them spiritual brothers in the Mahabharata.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, on whose chariot banner is Hanuman said to be present during the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: Hanuman is said to appear on Arjuna's banner during the Kurukshetra war, lending strength to his chariot.

questions.q: The Ramayana describes how an injury to Hanuman's jaw gives rise to his name, since 'hanu' refers to which part of the body?
questions.explanation: The name Hanuman is traditionally linked to 'hanu,' the jaw, recalling the injury he received as an infant.
